title: Postnatal Astrocyte Labeling by Electroporation (PALE)
author: Shiyi Wang
materials: Monitor pups until collection at P21.
Generate surface reconstructions of the astrocytes.
Analyze imaged astrocytes using Imaris Bitplane software.

steps.title: Anesthetize late P0/early P1 wild-type CD1 mice by hypothermia
steps.instruction: Anesthetize late P0/early P1 wild-type CD1 mice by hypothermia.

steps.title: Prepare plasmid DNA mixed with Fast Green Dye for visualization
steps.instruction: Prepare plasmid DNA mixed with Fast Green Dye for visualization. For shRNA knockdown experiments: Prepare 1 μl of DNA containing 1 μg of pGLAST-PBase and 1 μg of pPB-shRNA-mCherryCAAX for injection.

steps.title: Inject 1 μl of plasmid DNA...
steps.instruction: Inject 1 μl of plasmid DNA mixture into the lateral ventricle of one hemisphere using a pulled glass pipette (Drummond). Maintain sterile conditions during plasmid preparation and injection procedures.

steps.title: Orient electrodes with the positive terminal...
steps.instruction: Orient electrodes with the positive terminal above the visual cortex and the negative terminal below the chin of the pups.

steps.title: Apply 5 discrete 50 ms pulses...
steps.instruction: Apply 5 discrete 50 ms pulses of 100 V spaced 950 ms apart. Optimize electroporation parameters for consistent and reproducible results.

steps.title: Recover pups on a heating pad
steps.instruction: Recover pups on a heating pad. Mouse can go back to home cage once it has regained pink color and is breathing and moving around.

steps.title: Wash the brain sections in 1x TBS containing 0
steps.instruction: Wash the brain sections in 1x TBS containing 0.2% Triton X-100 (TBST). Use brain sections containing the visual cortex. Examine brain sections for the presence of electroporated cells before subsequent staining procedures.

steps.title: Block non-specific binding by incubating sections...
steps.instruction: Block non-specific binding by incubating sections in 10% normal goat serum (NGS) diluted in TBST for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Cut toe to identify mouse if needed
steps.instruction: Cut toe to identify mouse if needed.

steps.title: Record mouse ID and conditions
steps.instruction: Record mouse ID and conditions.

steps.title: Perform transcardial perfusion using TBS/Heparin followed by 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA)
steps.instruction: Perform transcardial perfusion using TBS/Heparin followed by 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA).

steps.title: Remove the brain and post-fix it...
steps.instruction: Remove the brain and post-fix it in 4% PFA Overnight at 4°C.

steps.title: Cryoprotect the brain in 30% sucrose solution until sunk
steps.instruction: Cryoprotect the brain in 30% sucrose solution until sunk.

steps.title: Section the brain into 100 μm...
steps.instruction: Section the brain into 100 μm thick coronal slices using a cryostat.

steps.title: Store the brain blocks at −80°C until sectioning
steps.instruction: Store the brain blocks at −80°C until sectioning.

steps.title: Embed the brain in a solution...
steps.instruction: Embed the brain in a solution containing 2 parts 30% sucrose and 1 part O.C.T. (TissueTek).

steps.title: Store the sections in a 1:1...
steps.instruction: Store the sections in a 1:1 mixture of TBS and glycerol at −20°C for further use.

steps.title: Acquire images using a fluorescence microscope (e
steps.instruction: Acquire images using a fluorescence microscope (e.g., Olympus FV 3000) with a 60x objective, 2x zoom to acquire high-magnification and resolution (0.5 μm step size) images of individual astrocytes. Criteria for inclusion: Capture the entire astrocyte within a single brain section.

steps.title: Seal the edges of the coverslip with nail polish
steps.instruction: Seal the edges of the coverslip with nail polish.

steps.title: Mount sections onto glass slides using...
steps.instruction: Mount sections onto glass slides using a homemade mounting media (90% Glycerol, 20 mM Tris pH 8.0, 0.5% n-Propyl gallate).

steps.title: Incubate sections in Alexa Fluor 594...
steps.instruction: Incubate sections in Alexa Fluor 594 goat anti-rabbit IgG(H+L) secondary antibodies diluted 1:200 in TBST, for 03:00:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Wash sections with TBST
steps.instruction: Wash sections with TBST.

steps.title: Incubate sections with rabbit anti-RFP primary...
steps.instruction: Incubate sections with rabbit anti-RFP primary antibody (1:1,000) diluted in blocking buffer (10% NGS in TBST) for 3 nights at 4 °C with gentle shaking.

steps.title: Wash sections with TBST (3x 10 min)
steps.instruction: Wash sections with TBST (3x 10 min). For nuclear staining, add DAPI (1:50,000) to the second wash.

steps.title: One by one, transfer sections first...
steps.instruction: One by one, transfer sections first to a container filled with 2/3 TBS, 1/3 ddH2O. Mount the sections (3 per slide) with the help of a clean brush on glass slides. Allow sections to dry for approximately 5-10 min.

steps.title: Important
steps.instruction: Important! Wait at least 02:00:00 before imaging. This time is required for the sections to settle and achieve accurate z-stack imaging.

steps.title: Use a nested t test or...
steps.instruction: Use a nested t test or nested two-way ANOVA followed by the Bonferroni posthoc test for statistical comparisons.

steps.title: Analyze astrocyte territory sizes from biological replicates across experimental conditions
steps.instruction: Analyze astrocyte territory sizes from biological replicates across experimental conditions.

steps.title: Use Imaris Xtensions “Visualize Surface Spots”...
steps.instruction: Use Imaris Xtensions “Visualize Surface Spots” and “Convex Hull” to create additional surface renders representing the territory of each astrocyte.

steps.title: Record the volume of each astrocyte territory
steps.instruction: Record the volume of each astrocyte territory.
